stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is 95%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is 94%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is between 67% and 87% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.598 to 0.777 as the panel discussion pass rate varies from 67% to 87%. Point estimate: 0.688.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the panel discussion stage creates a 17.9 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the panel discussion rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
